todos.text: Define OpenAPI schemas for terminal booking and terminal device
todos.details: Add TerminalBookingRequest schema (rfid_tag, pin, booking_type, terminal_id, local_timestamp, sequence_number) and TerminalDevice schema (id, name, location, api_key_hash, allowed_ips, status, last_heartbeat). Also add TerminalIdentifyResponse for employee lookup results.

todos.text: Define OpenAPI paths for real-time terminal endpoints
todos.details: Add POST /api/v1/terminal/identify (PIN+RFID lookup), POST /api/v1/terminal/bookings (single real-time booking), POST /api/v1/terminal/bookings/batch (offline queue sync), POST /api/v1/terminal/heartbeat (terminal status ping). All endpoints use API key auth header instead of JWT.

todos.text: Bundle OpenAPI spec and regenerate Go models
todos.details: Run `make swagger-bundle` then `make generate` to produce updated Go structs in gen/models/ that include the new terminal request/response types.

Implement middleware that validates X-Terminal-API-Key header against stored terminal keys, checks request source IP against the terminal's allowed_ips list, and injects terminal context (terminal_id, tenant_id) into the request context. Return 401 for invalid key, 403 for disallowed IP.

todos.text: Add terminal device DB model and repository
todos.details: Create GORM model for terminals table (id, tenant_id, name, location_id, api_key_hash, allowed_ips jsonb, is_active, last_heartbeat_at). Implement repository methods: FindByAPIKey, UpdateHeartbeat, ListByTenant, Create, Update, Delete.

todos.text: Create DB migration for terminals and employee RFID fields
todos.details: Run `make migrate-create name=add_terminals_and_rfid`. Create terminals table with columns matching the model. Add rfid_tag (unique per tenant) and pin_hash columns to employees table. Add index on (tenant_id, rfid_tag) and (tenant_id, api_key_hash).

todos.text: Implement terminal identify service and handler
todos.details: Service method IdentifyEmployee(tenantID, rfidTag, pin) that looks up employee by RFID tag within tenant, verifies PIN hash (bcrypt), returns employee name and current clock status. Handler parses request, calls service, returns TerminalIdentifyResponse. Reject if employee not found or PIN mismatch.

todos.text: Implement real-time terminal booking service and handler
todos.details: Handler POST /terminal/bookings accepts TerminalBookingRequest, validates booking_type enum, calls service. Service creates booking record with terminal_id as source, uses local_timestamp from terminal (not server time) to handle offline queue ordering. Apply idempotency check on (terminal_id + sequence_number) to prevent duplicate submissions during retry sync.

todos.text: Implement batch sync endpoint for offline queue
todos.details: Add BatchCreateTerminalBookings method that accepts array of terminal bookings, processes them in sequence_number order, skips duplicates by (terminal_id, sequence_number), and returns per-item success/error results so the terminal knows which items to remove from its local queue.

Handler POST /terminal/heartbeat updates last_heartbeat_at and accepts optional status payload (firmware_version, queue_depth, uptime). Returns server time so terminal can detect clock drift. This also serves as the connectivity check for offline detection.

Add a route group under /api/v1/terminal that uses the terminal auth middleware (not JWT middleware). Register identify, bookings, bookings/batch, and heartbeat routes. Keep existing /api/v1/bookings batch import routes unchanged for backward compatibility.

todos.text: Write integration tests for terminal booking flow
todos.details: Test full flow: register terminal → identify employee (valid PIN+RFID, invalid PIN, unknown RFID) → create booking (clock-in, duplicate rejection, invalid type) → batch sync (mixed valid/duplicate items) → heartbeat update. Test terminal auth middleware separately (valid key, invalid key, wrong IP).
